title: Lists: holding many things
body: A *list* holds many items in order: `[3, 7, 1]`. Grab one by position — `nums[0]` is the first, `nums[-1]` is the last (negative counts from the back). Add to the end with `nums.append(x)`, count items with `len(nums)`, and walk through them with `for x in nums:`. Lists show up everywhere from here on, so the next two challenges lock in indexing and appending.
